items.text: I had earlier instructed that after two minutes of inactivity, every node should collapse. I don't see that happening at all in the product. How can an instruction that was executed earlier and was successful earlier not be happening with the latest commits? Do you not refer to the earlier instructions, and if it was not satisfying the earlier instructions? How can I build better so that none of my earlier successful interactions or behaviors aren't wiped out? How can those be retained while I'm white coding through cursor?

I want you to go through all the chats from the last 2 weeks and make a compilation of all the UI and interaction and motion instructions I have given you, in terms of hover states, in terms of visibilities, in terms of smoothness and everything around it. An exhaustive set of instructions on these topics that I have given and compiled them into an MD file, and also create an HTML webpage for me to view it on a local host on a new port.

Include the icon, give some space, and make sure the pill is containing the contents properly. Right now, you can see there is extra space on the pill towards the right.We use iconography which belongs to the family of Flowstate. I don't need the time in decimals of seconds.Just seconds as the least count would be more than enough. In the top left collapsed state of the sidebar, in between the icon logo and the chevron, introduce the Canvas title. There are some cases whenever I add a new artifact or drop a new link of anything, the sidebar pops up. That is completely unnecessary and should never happen. I am unable to use the pencil. Once I click on it, the cursor should change to a pencil mode and should behave like a normal pencil on any digital tool works on the canvas. They should be able to draw anything. Command Z should work on it. Just think more on how to implement a pencil well. In the top right bar, there are chats and artifacts that are active or shown. Remove the text of chats and artifacts. This is where the iconography should work.
In the table, there is no affordance that I can resize the column width. Make sure those small little bars are visible. That's the indication that something can be interacted with,
Write all the suggestions from superbase and show them to me in a table.
for each of the suggestions, ask me a question whether I want to address that or not, or add an extra context.
yes, implement and show me the status at each point, preferably with iconography if you can.

Explore the Flowstate codebase at "d:\Cursor projects\Branch AI" for: (1) canvas toolbar tools pattern - how tools like shapes/text are added, (2) any existing drawing/pencil functionality, (3) HTML file import flow, (4) Twitter/X embed vs website embed handling. Return file paths, patterns to follow, and integration points for pencil tool, HTML import fix, and Twitter profile embed.

Explore the Flowstate codebase at "d:\Cursor projects\Branch AI" for view-only/read-only canvas mode and the Q shortcut for dropping questions. Find: how canvas edit vs view permissions work, keyboard shortcuts (especially Q), question dropping. Return file paths, permission checks, and where to block Q for viewers.

Explore the Flowstate codebase at "d:\Cursor projects\Branch AI" for the GitHub repository artifact/widget. Find: drag handles, central piece size, text size control, resize/grab areas. Return file paths and relevant props/styles for the 1.5x grab area and removing text size control.

Explore the Flowstate codebase at "d:\Cursor projects\Branch AI" to find how artifact drag-and-drop works from the toolbar/artifact popup. Find: toolbar components, drag ghost/preview during drag, artifact popup, canvas drop handling. Return file paths, key component/function names, and how drag state is managed. Be thorough - search for drag, ghost, toolbar, artifact popup, onDragStart, etc.

the same design language, in another just beside the invoice generator in another similar container. Build me a price calculator for an Indian freelancer with different kinds of deliverables, such as: - websites - design systems - mobile app designs and build a custom price calculator for them.
What formats does my custom UI component support? Also, what are the conditions for which a custom UI component is spun up?
the invoice should always be an A4-sized invoice, and just make sure the title "Riviga website design" is light in color.
npx skills add https://github.com/dimillian/skills --skill swiftui-ui-patterns I will use this skill and redesign the entire UI only from scratch.

npx skills add https://github.com/bencium/bencium-marketplace --skill bencium-innovative-ux-designeruse this skill and redesign everything from scratch again. Make it responsive. I just want the PDF or the editable document at the bottom and all the controls at the top. It should overall be a more height and less width rectangular form. That's how I want my main component to be, which contains both the controls, which have the export or font or other controls, and the editable document below.

Remove the controls of layout and view. Only font, just + or -, should be there. Remove the slider. The actions of remove row should be inside the table itself, which is already there, so no need to have it again there in actions. I just want a download icon instead of a huge button. Beside it, a button saying "Reset", and just beside it, font controls: font controls first, then reset, then download. These are the only three controls that have to be there in the first line. Also add currency selector in the first line itself. In fact, that's the only line with controls.

- Currency should be to the leftmost, and no need of a title. - After that should come the font controls for that, just receive the controls by small a and big A icon. - To the right come reset first, and then download. - Later add a download CTA copy, and make the button the color of the icon and the icon stroke white.

make sure whatever content is there in the invoice fits the height and is responsive to the height of all the elements inside the invoice template. The footer should always come at the bottom in its fixed size. In the middle section, project details, after the line and before the container ends, you can just make that container of project details as a whole responsive in height.
Give me just a simple table of all the set API keys.I want you to give the keys visible in the table. It's ok, I'll take care.

Remove the keyboard shortcut S for sticky notes. Retain it in the PI menu.Also make sure the colors look good in dark theme. Also, if I hit Enter while inside the sticky notes or click on Done inside this toolbar of the sticky notes, I should be coming back to the artifact selected stage. Make sure the hover functionality acts exactly similarly to other artifacts in the toolbar. I want the font size controller and the edit button: either on clicking on edit or while double-clicking on the sticky note, anyone should be able to edit the text inside. Upon editing something, the edit button becomes Save. Upon clicking on Save, the artifact should save the edited text inside those two keynotes.

For each question, when it is loading the answer along with the three dots and the copy below it, underneath them I want you to add two more data points: - The time it is taking to give the response. This should be a timer that starts running from zero after the user hits enter or sends the question or submits the question - Starting tokens being consumed in real time for this particular query

Create a custom artifact which has a search functionality for a location. When a location is searched for, it should zoom into that location. This map styling is such that only roads are visible; the rest is white. It should generate the map with all the points of interest and everything else other than roads to be nonexistent. Only roads are to be present in black. I want an interactive map. Use OSM for the map. Prove this prompt for me and give me

Whenever an artefact is being updated through another chat and it is in progress of its next version, it should show a glowing, moving, subtle stroke which is pleasant to watch.This should only be for the output artefacts.

I want to put a hard limit of 3 minutes. If any response or artifact takes more than 3 minutes, it should go into a failure state. It should stop any active work happening on that artifact or answer and go into the state which shows "Try again".

I want to add a feature in the beta suggestion popup. - I want to add a CTA for "Capture Screenshot", which captures the screenshot of the app in its current state and attaches it as an image. - Remove the "Copy/Paste/Drag or Drop Images into Textbox". - Move "Cancel" and "Submit" to the bottom of the card. - Move "Send as Prem Pradeep email" above the two CTS of "Cancel" and "Submit". - "Attach Image" and "Capture Screenshot" should be pills under the textbox. - The pills should be horizontally stacked in the parent container containing them.

On the top right, to the left of suggestions, I want to see a stat of how many chats are currently in progress and how many artifacts are currently being created. At the moment, I want this to be a live tracker. It should only look at the truth whether a new question is dropped and we are yet to know the success or failure of the answer.It counts as in progress. And if an artifact is in the state of a generation or it's being built, it is counted as in progress. After a chat is successfully completed or after an artifact is successfully completed, both of them vanish. When I say successfully completed, it could even be an absolute failure case as well, which is truthful. In these cases, they cease to count as in progress.Build me this live drakkar, which is just a show of in progress of chats and artifacts.

In flowstate, for someone to build a custom UI successfully in the way they want, I realized just a single agent is not enough. There should be multiple agents running multiple different tasks to finally arrive at the custom UI. I don't know how to build that architecture of creating subagents. Can you help me with it?Just focus on educating me right now. There is no need to create a plan to build. Just educate me.

Every artefact on the canvas should be copyable, be it empty or filled. Question and answer nodes should not be copyable. Only input artefacts, output artefacts, and skills should be copyable. The ways of copying could be: - The usual keyboard shortcuts for Mac and Windows - Right click and, in the current options, another third option of copy should come - Alt drag should be able to copy the compnents Any component or artifact a person copies should be pasteable or be able to paste in any other canvas. Make sure the artifact outer container and the version history are also copied.

Canvas artifact & skill copy/paste Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself. To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.
Command C and Command V or Ctrl C and Ctrl V are not working for artifacts. Investigate why. Alt and drag is working perfectly.

whenever something is copied and it is copied successfully, be it HTML code or just artifacts or other components, I want a small toast message at the top middle that just comes and goes. Also, whenever an error message comes up, make sure it is dismissable, and right now the dismiss button is too ugly. Just add a close button for errors and success messages like "artifact copied", "image copied", Or any successful copy I should give a toast message.
it seems like you are stuck at waiting for shell. Please rectify whatever is causing you the blocker.

I want to fine-tune my pitch for Flowstate because right now it's becoming too random when I'm introducing it casually in conversations. My ideal flow would be to start with the restricted nature of chat interfaces, but even before that I want to start with the general usage of AI among everyone and how crazy it is that people are building mini micro tools for themselves. Right now they exist either in the chats or websites that they are posting. I would then want to talk about the limited restriction around chat interface while you're trying to learn something or research about something, or actually trying to build something. Then I want to introduce Flowstate verbally and say that it is a canvas-based AI tool which lets me run multiple threads and is fundamentally output-first and not chat-first, so that you can focus on what you are creating or working on. Flowstate lets you move your artifacts around on the canvas, invite collaborators to the canvas so that they can co-create with you and share your created artifacts with other folks. Flowstate gets you the power of AI into a much more user-friendly place where you don't have to post websites or get into complex code for creating small tools and sharing them with relevant people. Flowstate lets you create complex tools right on the canvas, edit them, modify them, and share them with other people. Then I open Flowstate, and I want to start with typing in a simple question, starting with a user scenario of probably travel. Let's say I want to travel to London at the end of the year, and I type in my travel details. As I type in London, because the map artifact can intelligently guess when to show up, as the answer is typing, I immediately see an artifact pop-up of the map. Parallelly, I want to ask two-three other questions which are relevant to my travel and which can create a table, which can fetch images, which can add documents onto the canvas and create custom components which could be useful to me. I later want to talk about the customizations that one can do to their canvas. I want to end with the possible use cases one can use Flowstate for. I also want to show collaboration live on the canvas before I move into the canvas customizations. I wanted to invite someone and have them ask questions on the same thing and on the same canvas and pull connectors from the artifacts that we've created and update them.I want you to create a simple table for me which breaks this flow of demo into multiple stages. - I want to have a serial number. - I want to have a title. - I want to have the description. - I want to have the script. - I want to have a column for Flowstate visuals where I show specifically what I do in the demo. - Actually, make product features another column.

I want to note some use cases which have a magic factor. Add this as a different section in the document. - A map popping up whenever I mention a name is magic. - The name of a city is magic. - After a map has popped up, if I ask it to add markers to the map, it showing a version 2 of the map with the markers on the map is magical. - When I mention a location's name, it's showing a street view is magical. - When I mention a place in the map, it's showing a street view is magical. Being able to copy an artefact is magical because it makes it feel extremely light. When I created a graph out of my chat and then I asked a follow-up question and I edited the graph, that was magical. The canvas becoming a moving space is magical. Transparency GIFs is magical. The quick explain is magical. I want to create a list of some basic features which also have to be highlighted: - Branching from a chat - Switching to chat view from branch view - Adding any kind of attachments onto the canvas
In the left side bar, when I am on my current Canvas and I double click on it, I should be able to rename the Canvas. When I hit Enter, it should rename the Canvas successfully.

Also, can you add the ability to select multiple objects and group them together? The group CTA currently exists, but it doesn't do anything. When I group something together, it should form a visible bounding box over it, and that bounding box should be nameable. It should have an editable name. The default title should be "Untitled Group". This new group should act as an artifact which contains the context of all of the contents inside that group. I should be able to hover and pull a connector from this group, and all the questions and responses or artifacts go as attachments and as text input to the new question that is being added. Will this considerably increase the token limit? Can you tell me other alternatives for executing this?
Give me a graph of the number of new features built per day in the last one month for flowstate. Also, in the same bar graph, show me the number of bug fixes as well per day.
Show me the Flowstate website that you have built. Run it on a local host.

Me and my collaborator on the canvas of Flowstate have just observed that the current two portions, along with some added images onto the canvas, have just vanished upon reload. This should never happen. This is a critical bug. It vanished for my collaborator and for me on the canvas. Observe why this is happening. Check the recent logs of Flowstate. Prem Pradeep and Ravi, understand why the history was lost. There should be nothing lost ever upon reload. Make sure you build robust systems to make sure this doesn't happen.

Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.

# Repository Explorer V1 - Technical Design Document ## Objective Repository Explorer helps users evaluate an open-source GitHub repository in under 2 minutes. Users paste a GitHub repository URL. The system automatically collects information from GitHub, README files, repository assets, deployment links, package manifests, and creator profiles, then presents the repository as a visually rich project overview. The goal is not code analysis. The goal is rapid project evaluation. --- # User Flow 1. User pastes GitHub URL 2. Backend fetches repository metadata 3. System discovers assets, previews, stack, dependencies, and creator information 4. Claude generates structured summaries 5. UI renders repository dashboard Target response time: * Initial load: YouTube links Video embeds Store links Architecture image names: architecture system design workflow --- ## 3. Preview Widget Purpose: Allow users to try the project immediately. ### Detection Sources GitHub Pages Vercel Netlify Cloudflare Pages Render Railway Firebase Hosting Static HTML Storybook Documentation Sites ### Data Points Preview Available Preview URL Preview Type Deployment Provider Preview Confidence Score ### Preview Types GitHub Pages Static Website Storybook Documentation Hosted Application Mobile Only Desktop Only No Preview ### Render Strategy If URL available: Embed in iframe Else: Show deployment details --- ## 4. Tech Details Widget Purpose: Provide technical understanding without reading source code. ### Stack Detection Frontend Backend Database Infrastructure AI Providers Mobile Framework Desktop Framework ### Sources package.json requirements.txt pyproject.toml Cargo.toml go.mod Gemfile Dockerfile docker-compose .github workflows ### Data Points Frontend Framework Backend Framework Database Hosting Platform Authentication Provider Payment Provider AI Provider Programming Languages ### Dependencies OpenAI Anthropic Stripe Firebase Supabase AWS Azure Twilio Resend Pinecone Weaviate Qdrant ### Architecture Claude generates: System Architecture Summary Example: Browser → Backend → PostgreSQL → OpenAI ### Setup Installation Commands Docker Support Environment Variables Required Estimated Setup Time --- ## 5. Built By Widget Purpose: Provide creator credibility and context. ### Sources Repository Owner GitHub Profile Organization Profile README Personal Website LinkedIn Twitter/X ### Data Points Creator Name Avatar GitHub Profile Website Organization Location (if public) Bio Follower Count Public Repository Count ### Additional Context Top Repositories Other Popular Projects Years Active --- # Repository Classification Claude generates: Category Examples: AI Application Frontend Library Backend Framework Mobile App Design System Developer Tool Browser Extension CLI Tool Desktop Application Game Dataset Research Project Hardware Project Automation Tool --- # Backend Architecture Frontend Next.js TypeScript Tailwind Backend Node.js Express or Next.js API Routes Database PostgreSQL Caching Redis --- # Data Collection Pipeline Step 1 Fetch GitHub Repository Metadata GitHub REST API Step 2 Fetch README Step 3 Fetch Repository Tree Step 4 Identify Key Files package.json requirements.txt Dockerfile README docs public assets screenshots Step 5 Extract Media Images Videos GIFs Preview URLs Step 6 Generate Structured Context Step 7 Send To Claude Prompt Claude to produce: Overview Tech Summary Architecture Tags Audience Step 8 Render Dashboard --- # Claude Output Schema { overview: {}, media: {}, preview: {}, techDetails: {}, builtBy: {} } Claude should return strict JSON. No markdown. No prose outside schema. --- # Success Metric A user should be able to answer: What is this? What does it look like? Can I try it? How does it work? I want smooth appear-in animations at a lot of places as a motion design family in my product. - Whenever one of the sidebars is being expanded, I wanted to follow a specific Bezier curve based on the visual weight of the component being moved. - Whenever a new question is dropped, I wanted to drop on the canvas with a smooth motion where it drops, then scales down, scales up, and settles, like there is a small bounce, like it is magnetically stuck on to the canvas. - When a new branch is popped up or an artefact is popping up or a follow-up node is popping up, I wanted to slide in and appear from zero opacity to full opacity, slide in from the bottom, and follow a similar family smooth Bezier curve. - When the landing page loads, all the elements should appear sliding in from the bottom, not at the same time but with a small offset delay after each element. Overall, the animation should take less than one second for the landing page to appear, but there should be minor offsets in the elements that are appearing sequentially.Take micro decisions like there should be slightly longer gap between sections. For example, the title is a specific section, and then the artifact solutions are a specific section, and the search bar is a section, and then the tips below are another section. There can be a slightly longer delay between the sections appearing as well.Plan for the motion and overall app study motion design systems of Gojek and other good software, and act like a motion design lead for a software product. Ask me more questions on where else motion can be applied, how I want to standardize these, and what parameters I need to standardize. I want extreme detail in the work, and I want good documentation of the motion design language of flowstate.

You have to always make sure motion doesn't happen at the cost of performance. The motion should be optimized to run with lesser power. Be smart with the code and the animations and use the libraries and methods which take the least processing power.

import React, { useEffect, useRef } from 'react'; // --- FRAGMENT SHADER --- // We add a `u_color` uniform to accept a color from our component. const fragmentShaderSource = `#version 300 es precision highp float; out vec4 O; uniform float time; uniform vec2 resolution; uniform vec3 u_color; // { const result = /^#?([a-f\d]{2})([a-f\d]{2})([a-f\d]{2})$/i.exec(hex); return result ? [ parseInt(result[1], 16) / 255, parseInt(result[2], 16) / 255, parseInt(result[3], 16) / 255, ] : null; }; // --- REACT COMPONENT --- interface AnimatedBackgroundProps { smokeColor?: string; // e.g., "#8A2BE2" } export const SmokeBackground: React.FC = ({ smokeColor = "#808080" // Default to gray }) => { const canvasRef = useRef (null); const rendererRef = useRef (null); // Effect for initialization and cleanup useEffect(() => { if (!canvasRef.current) return; const canvas = canvasRef.current; const renderer = new Renderer(canvas, fragmentShaderSource); rendererRef.current = renderer; const handleResize = () => renderer.updateScale(); handleResize(); // Initial size window.addEventListener('resize', handleResize); let animationFrameId: number; const loop = (now: number) => { renderer.render(now); animationFrameId = requestAnimationFrame(loop); }; loop(0); return () => { window.removeEventListener('resize', handleResize); cancelAnimationFrame(animationFrameId); renderer.reset(); }; }, []); // Effect to update color when the prop changes useEffect(() => { const renderer = rendererRef.current; if (renderer) { const rgbColor = hexToRgb(smokeColor); if (rgbColor) { renderer.updateColor(rgbColor); } } }, [smokeColor]); return ( ); }; Add this as another canvas background. The Kernel and Brew By Numbers are both based in Bermondsey, which has become a hub for London's craft beer culture — their "Bermondsey Beer Mile" is well worth a visit! Beavertown is one of the most recognisable craft brands in the UK, known for their bold flavours and striking skull-themed can art.
